name: Volodymyr Zelenskyy
bio: President of Ukraine since May 2019, previously known as a comedian and actor who starred in the television series Servant of the People. He has led Ukraine through the full-scale Russian invasion that began in February 2022, becoming a prominent wartime leader on the global stage. Zelenskyy has addressed numerous international bodies to rally support for Ukrainian sovereignty and defense.

name: Donald Trump
bio: 45th and 47th President of the United States, serving from 2017 to 2021 and again from January 2025. A businessman and media personality before entering politics, he won the 2024 presidential election to become the second president in American history to serve non-consecutive terms. His political platform centers on immigration enforcement, trade protectionism, and an America First foreign policy.

name: Joe Biden
bio: 46th President of the United States, serving from January 2021 to January 2025. Previously served as Vice President under Barack Obama from 2009 to 2017 and represented Delaware in the U.S. Senate for 36 years. His presidency focused on COVID-19 recovery, infrastructure investment, and reinforcing transatlantic alliances.

name: Vladimir Putin
bio: President of Russia since 2000, with a brief interlude as Prime Minister from 2008 to 2012. A former KGB intelligence officer, he has dominated Russian politics for over two decades and centralized executive power. He launched the full-scale invasion of Ukraine in February 2022, fundamentally reshaping European security.

name: Xi Jinping
role: President / General Secretary of the CPC
bio: General Secretary of the Chinese Communist Party since 2012 and President of the People's Republic of China since 2013. He has consolidated power to a degree unseen since Mao Zedong, eliminating presidential term limits in 2018. His tenure has emphasized national rejuvenation, technological self-reliance, and an assertive foreign policy.

name: Emmanuel Macron
bio: President of France since May 2017, the youngest president in French history at the time of his election at age 39. A former investment banker and economy minister, he founded the centrist party En Marche (now Renaissance) and won re-election in 2022. He has positioned himself as a leading voice for European strategic autonomy and multilateral cooperation.

name: Narendra Modi
bio: Prime Minister of India since May 2014, leading the Bharatiya Janata Party to three consecutive general election victories. Previously served as Chief Minister of Gujarat from 2001 to 2014, overseeing rapid economic growth in the state. His tenure has focused on economic modernization, digital infrastructure, and elevating India's role in global affairs.

name: Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva
bio: President of Brazil serving his third term since January 2023, having previously served from 2003 to 2010. A former metalworker and union leader, he co-founded the Workers' Party and led Brazil through a period of significant economic growth and poverty reduction during his first two terms. His return to power followed a contentious legal saga and a narrow election victory over incumbent Jair Bolsonaro.

name: Cyril Ramaphosa
bio: President of South Africa since February 2018, succeeding Jacob Zuma. A former trade union leader and key negotiator during the transition from apartheid, he later became one of South Africa's wealthiest businessmen. He has focused on anti-corruption reforms and economic recovery while navigating coalition politics after the ANC lost its parliamentary majority in 2024.

name: Javier Milei
bio: President of Argentina since December 2023, a libertarian economist who won office as a political outsider campaigning against what he called the political caste. Known for his dramatic rhetorical style and radical free-market proposals, including dollarization and the elimination of government ministries. His presidency represents a sharp ideological shift in Argentine politics toward economic deregulation and fiscal austerity.

name: Ali Khamenei
bio: Supreme Leader of Iran since 1989, succeeding Ruhollah Khomeini as the highest political and religious authority in the Islamic Republic. Previously served as President of Iran from 1981 to 1989. He holds ultimate authority over Iran's foreign policy, military, and nuclear program, and has overseen the country through decades of tensions with Western powers.

name: Ebrahim Raisi
bio: President of Iran from August 2021 until his death in a helicopter crash in May 2024. A conservative cleric and jurist, he previously served as head of Iran's judiciary and was sanctioned by Western governments for human rights abuses. His presidency saw the collapse of the JCPOA nuclear negotiations and a deepening of Iran's alignment with Russia and China.

name: Ahmed al-Sharaa
bio: President of Syria following the fall of the Assad regime in December 2024, previously known by his nom de guerre Abu Mohammed al-Julani. He led Hayat Tahrir al-Sham and the broader opposition coalition that toppled Bashar al-Assad's government after a rapid military advance. He has sought to present himself as a pragmatic leader focused on rebuilding Syria and establishing relations with the international community.

name: Benjamin Netanyahu
bio: Prime Minister of Israel and the longest-serving leader in Israeli history, currently serving his sixth government since returning to power in December 2022. Previously served as Prime Minister from 1996 to 1999 and from 2009 to 2021. His current term has been defined by the October 7, 2023 Hamas attack and the subsequent military operations in Gaza, as well as his ongoing corruption trial.

name: António Guterres
bio: Secretary-General of the United Nations since January 2017, serving his second term. Previously served as Prime Minister of Portugal from 1995 to 2002 and as United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees from 2005 to 2015. He has focused on climate action, conflict prevention, and reform of the multilateral system.

name: Jerome Powell
bio: Chair of the Federal Reserve since February 2018, appointed by President Trump and reappointed by President Biden. A lawyer and former investment banker, he joined the Fed's Board of Governors in 2012. He has navigated the central bank through the COVID-19 economic crisis, the subsequent surge in inflation, and the most aggressive interest rate hiking cycle in four decades.

name: Christine Lagarde
bio: President of the European Central Bank since November 2019, the first woman to hold the position. Previously served as Managing Director of the International Monetary Fund from 2011 to 2019 and as France's Minister of Finance. She has steered eurozone monetary policy through the pandemic and the subsequent energy and inflation crisis.

name: Kristalina Georgieva
bio: Managing Director of the International Monetary Fund since October 2019, the first person from an emerging market economy to lead the institution. Previously served as CEO of the World Bank and as European Commissioner for Budget and Human Resources. She has overseen the IMF's response to the pandemic, global debt challenges, and the economic fallout from geopolitical fragmentation.

name: Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala
bio: Director-General of the World Trade Organization since March 2021, the first woman and first African to lead the organization. Previously served twice as Nigeria's Finance Minister and spent 25 years at the World Bank, rising to Managing Director. She has worked to revitalize the WTO's negotiating function and address issues including pandemic preparedness and fisheries subsidies.

name: Yi Gang
bio: Governor of the People's Bank of China from 2018 to 2023, overseeing Chinese monetary policy during a period of significant economic transition. An economist educated at the University of Illinois, he previously taught at Indiana University before returning to China to join the PBOC. He managed the delicate balance of supporting economic growth while controlling financial risks during the pandemic and the property sector crisis.

name: Andrew Bailey
bio: Governor of the Bank of England since March 2020, taking office just as the COVID-19 pandemic struck. Previously served as Chief Executive of the Financial Conduct Authority and spent three decades at the Bank of England in various roles. He has navigated British monetary policy through the pandemic, the energy price shock, and the gilt market crisis following the 2022 mini-budget.

name: Janet Yellen
bio: United States Secretary of the Treasury from January 2021 to January 2025, the first woman to hold the position. Previously served as Chair of the Federal Reserve from 2014 to 2018, also the first woman in that role. A labor economist by training, she managed Treasury policy through pandemic recovery, inflation challenges, and the implementation of major fiscal legislation.

name: Nirmala Sitharaman
bio: Minister of Finance and Corporate Affairs of India since 2019, the first full-time female finance minister of the country. Previously served as Minister of Defence from 2017 to 2019 and as Minister of Commerce and Industry. She has presented multiple Union Budgets focused on infrastructure investment, digital economy expansion, and fiscal consolidation.

name: Ajay Banga
bio: President of the World Bank Group since June 2023, nominated by President Biden. Previously served as Executive Chairman and CEO of Mastercard from 2010 to 2021, where he led the company's expansion into digital payments across emerging markets. He has focused on reforming the World Bank to address climate change alongside poverty reduction and to mobilize greater private sector investment.
